raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need a bash command that searches all files in /workspace, sorts by depth (i.e., number of directories from root). Depth: root /workspace is depth 0, its subfolders depth 1, etc. We can use find /workspace -type f -printf "%d %p\n" then sort -n. But need only file names. So maybe: find /workspace -type f -printf "%d %p\n" | sort -n | cut -d' ' -f2- . That prints full path. If just names relative? Probably full path. Provide one line.
